context: Every story needs a when. Before you learn the past-tense verbs, get the time words that tell the listener you are talking about the past - yesterday, last night, last week. Drop one at the start of a sentence and everyone knows you have left the present.
explanation: Start with the easy anchors you know: ayer = yesterday, and hoy = today for contrast. Then add anoche = last night. These go at the front: ayer fui al gimnasio = yesterday I went to the gym. The pattern for "last ..." comes next with the word pasado.

examples.en: yesterday I went to the gym
examples.note: ayer at the front signals the past.

examples.en: last night I watched the World Cup

examples.es: la semana pasada fui a Albir
examples.en: last week I went to Albir
examples.note: la semana pasada = last week; pasada = last/past.

examples.es: el fin de semana pasado fui a la playa
examples.en: last weekend I went to the beach
examples.note: el fin de semana pasado - pasado agrees with the masculine fin de semana.

examples.es: el año pasado fui a Albir muchas veces
examples.en: last year I went to Albir many times
examples.note: el año pasado = last year; muchas veces = many times.
